text: Would you like to {{c1::grab a drink}} sometime?
explanation: grab a drink is a casual, friendly way to invite someone to go out for a beverage (coffee, beer, or cocktails).
usage:
Pattern: grab a [item] (e.g. grab a coffee, grab a bite to eat, grab lunch)
  • Let's grab a coffee this afternoon.
  • Do you want to grab a bite to eat after work?
spanish: ¿Te gustaría ir a tomar algo alguna vez?

text: I had a great time tonight, we should {{c1::do this again}}.
explanation: do this again is a friendly, low-pressure phrase used at the end of a good social gathering or date to show interest in hanging out again.
usage:
Pattern: we should do this again [sometime/soon]
  • I really enjoyed the museum. We should do this again sometime!
  • It was great catching up. We should do this again soon.
spanish: La pasé genial esta noche, deberíamos repetir esto.

text: Would you like to go out for a cup of coffee sometime next week, or perhaps grab a {{c1::bite to eat}}?
explanation: To 'grab a bite to eat' is a highly common, informal idiom meaning to get a quick meal or snack together.
usage:
Pattern: grab a bite to eat / get lunch
  • Let's grab a bite to eat before the movie starts.
  • I only have fifteen minutes to grab a bite to eat.
spanish: ¿Te gustaría salir a tomar una taza de café en algún momento de la próxima semana, o tal vez comer algo rápido?

text: I had a wonderful time tonight; thank you for showing me your favorite spot in the {{c1::city}}.
explanation: A 'favorite spot' refers to a person's preferred place (like a cafe, park view, or hidden bar) for leisure or dining.
usage:
Pattern: favorite spot / local hangout
  • This coffee shop is my favorite spot for reading.
  • She showed me her favorite spot in the botanical gardens.
spanish: La pasé de maravilla esta noche; gracias por mostrarme tu lugar favorito en la ciudad.

text: Let me know when you are free; I'd love to take you out to that new Italian restaurant we talked {{c1::about}}.
explanation: To 'talk about' something is the phrasal verb meaning to discuss a topic or place in conversation.
usage:
Pattern: talk about [place / topic]
  • This is the book I was talking about yesterday.
  • We talked about the database design for hours.
spanish: Avísame cuando estés libre; me encantaría llevarte a ese nuevo restaurante italiano del que hablamos.

text: I really enjoy talking to you; we seem to have a lot of common interests and share a similar {{c1::sense of humor}}.
explanation: A 'sense of humor' is a person's capability to perceive, appreciate, or express what is funny or amusing.
usage:
Pattern: sense of humor / witty / dry humor
  • He has a dry sense of humor that is hard to understand at first.
  • A shared sense of humor is key to a good relationship.
spanish: Realmente disfruto hablar contigo; parece que tenemos muchos intereses en común y compartimos un sentido del humor similar.

text: Are you free this weekend, or do you already have plans with your {{c1::friends}}?
explanation: To 'have plans' means to have pre-arranged activities or commitments with friends or family.
usage:
Pattern: have plans / busy this weekend
  • I'd love to come, but I already have plans.
  • Do you have plans for the holidays?
spanish: ¿Estás libre este fin de semana, o ya tienes planes con tus amigos?

text: I'd love to see you again; let's keep in touch and schedule something for next {{c1::weekend}}.
explanation: To 'keep in touch' is an idiom meaning to maintain communication or contact with someone over time.
usage:
Pattern: keep in touch / stay in contact
  • We promised to keep in touch after graduation.
  • Let's keep in touch via email.
spanish: Me encantaría verte de nuevo; mantengámonos en contacto y programemos algo para el próximo fin de semana.

text: Thank you for the dinner; it was very kind of you to treat me, and I'd like to pay next {{c1::time}}.
explanation: To 'pay next time' is a polite dating convention indicating that the recipient of a free meal wants to reciprocate by paying for the next date.
usage:
Pattern: pay next time / my treat next time
  • You paid today, so let me pay next time.
  • She insisted on paying next time.
spanish: Gracias por la cena; fue muy amable de tu parte invitarme, y me gustaría pagar la próxima vez.

text: Let's go for a walk in the park; it's a beautiful day and a great opportunity to chat and get to {{c1::know each other better}}.
explanation: To 'get to know each other better' means to learn more about a person's life, opinions, and character through conversation and shared experiences.
usage:
Pattern: get to know each other better
  • Dating is a process of getting to know each other better.
  • I want to get to know my teammates better.
spanish: Vamos a dar un paseo por el parque; es un hermoso día y una gran oportunidad para charlar y conocernos mejor.

text: Would you like to grab a drink after work this Friday? I know a great little place near the {{c1::office}}.
explanation: The 'office' is the physical workplace where colleagues work, commonly used as a reference point for post-work socializing.
usage:
Pattern: near the office / head to the office
  • Let's meet at the pub near the office.
  • I am working from the office today.
spanish: ¿Te gustaría ir a tomar un trago después del trabajo este viernes? Conozco un gran lugarcito cerca de la oficina.

text: I had a wonderful time chatting with you tonight; thank you for the lovely conversation and the {{c1::company}}.
explanation: 'Company' in this social context refers to the presence of another person or group, here appreciated at the end of a date.
usage:
Pattern: enjoy the company / keep someone company
  • Thank you for keeping me company while I waited for the train.
  • I really enjoy your company.
spanish: Me divertí mucho charlando contigo esta noche; gracias por la agradable conversación y la compañía.

text: Let me know when you are free next week; I'd love to take you to that new art gallery that just {{c1::opened}}.
explanation: To 'open' (or be newly opened) is the verb describing a new business, exhibition, or venue welcoming the public for the first time.
usage:
Pattern: just opened / newly opened
  • A new coffee shop just opened around the corner.
  • The museum opened its doors to the public yesterday.
spanish: Avísame cuando estés libre la próxima semana; me encantaría llevarte a esa nueva galería de arte que acaba de abrir.

text: I really enjoy spending time with you; you are very easy to talk to and have a great {{c1::personality}}.
explanation: A 'personality' is the combination of characteristics, qualities, and behaviors that form an individual's distinctive character.
usage:
Pattern: great personality / outgoing personality
  • She has a very warm and outgoing personality.
  • I like him because of his great personality.
spanish: Realmente disfruto pasar tiempo contigo; es muy fácil hablar contigo y tienes una gran personalidad.

text: Are you busy this weekend, or do you have some free time to hang out and grab a {{c1::bite to eat}}?
explanation: To 'grab a bite to eat' is a common informal idiom meaning to get a quick meal together, used here to invite someone on a casual date.
usage:
Pattern: grab a bite to eat / get food
  • Let's grab a bite to eat after the movie.
  • I need to grab a quick bite to eat.
spanish: ¿Estás ocupado este fin de semana, o tienes algo de tiempo libre para pasar el rato y comer algo rápido?

text: I'd love to see you again soon; let's keep in touch and plan something for next {{c1::Saturday}}.
explanation: 'Saturday' is the first day of the weekend, the most common day for arranging social dates or events.
usage:
Pattern: next Saturday / Saturday night
  • Are you free next Saturday afternoon?
  • We went to a concert last Saturday.
spanish: Me encantaría verte de nuevo pronto; mantengámonos en contacto y planifiquemos algo para el próximo sábado.

text: Thank you for the coffee; it was very nice of you to pay, and I'll treat you next {{c1::time}}.
explanation: To 'treat' someone next 'time' means to pay for their drink, meal, or ticket on the subsequent occasion as a reciprocal gesture.
usage:
Pattern: treat you next time / my treat next time
  • Put your wallet away; it's my treat today, and you can treat me next time.
  • She treated us to dinner yesterday.
spanish: Gracias por el café; fue muy amable de tu parte pagar, y yo te invito la próxima vez.

text: Let's go for a walk by the river; it's a beautiful evening and a nice opportunity to chat and get to {{c1::know each other better}}.
explanation: To 'get to know each other better' is the standard phrase for learning more about a romantic partner's background, tastes, and values.
usage:
Pattern: get to know each other better / build a connection
  • We spent the evening talking and getting to know each other better.
  • Our goal is to get to know our clients better.
spanish: Vamos a dar un paseo junto al río; es una hermosa tarde y una buena oportunidad para charlar y conocernos mejor.

text: I'll text you when I get home to let you know I arrived safely; thank you for a wonderful {{c1::date}}.
explanation: A 'date' is a pre-arranged social or romantic meeting, especially between two people who are interested in each other.
usage:
Pattern: first date / go on a date
  • Our first date was at a small sushi restaurant.
  • He asked her out on a date.
spanish: Te enviaré un mensaje de texto cuando llegue a casa para avisarte que llegué bien; gracias por una cita maravillosa.

text: Would you like to join me for a concert next month? I have an extra ticket and would love for you to {{c1::come along}}.
explanation: To 'come along' is a phrasal verb meaning to accompany someone, go with them, or join them on a trip or event.
usage:
Pattern: come along / join in
  • We are going to the beach; do you want to come along?
  • She invited me to come along to the gallery.
spanish: ¿Te gustaría acompañarme a un concierto el próximo mes? Tengo una entrada extra y me encantaría que me acompañaras.
